name: Typer
what_it_does: Python CLI building library that uses type hints to automatically generate argument parsers, help text, and shell completion, built on top of Click.
use_cases: Building agent CLI tools with typed parameters and auto-generated --help documentation with zero boilerplate
Creating multi-command CLI wrappers around agent workflows (e.g., run, evaluate, export) with subcommand routing
Exposing agent configuration via typed CLI arguments with validation, defaults, and environment variable overrides
Building operator tooling for managing agent deployments where type safety prevents misconfiguration at invocation time
Generating shell completion scripts for agent CLI tools to improve developer experience in bash/zsh environments
not_for: Interactive TUI applications with menus, forms, or real-time terminal UI — use Textual or Prompt Toolkit instead
Simple one-command scripts where argparse or plain sys.argv is sufficient and adding a dependency is overhead
Projects requiring Click features not yet exposed by Typer's abstraction layer where direct Click usage is cleaner
best_when: You are building Python CLI tools or agent operator interfaces and want type-safe argument parsing with minimal boilerplate using Python type hints.
avoid_when: You need fine-grained control over Click internals or are building complex interactive prompts that Typer's abstraction does not expose cleanly.

auth.notes: Local Python library, no auth required

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Type validation errors are clear and user-friendly; missing required arguments produce readable messages; internal Click errors can surface with less context
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CLI parsing is deterministic and stateless; idempotency of the invoked command depends on the application logic
known_agent_gotchas: Typer converts Optional[str] to a required argument unless a default=None is explicitly provided — type annotations alone are not enough to make arguments optional
Boolean flags require Annotated[bool, typer.Option('--flag/--no-flag')] syntax; bare bool type hints do not produce toggle flags automatically
Subcommand apps composed with app.add_typer() do not inherit callback context — each subapp must redeclare shared options or use a shared state object
Typer wraps Click under the hood so Click-specific behaviors (like multiple=True for list arguments) require typer.Option with List[str] type annotation to work correctly
Testing Typer CLIs requires typer.testing.CliRunner which behaves differently from Click's runner for exception propagation and exit code handling

security_score_components.security_notes: No network layer; secure by default. Avoid passing secrets as CLI arguments — they appear in process lists and shell history; prefer environment variables.
